title: Today's AI Brief: Microsoft offers devs a better way to control AI agent behavior
summary: Microsoft helps devs control AI agent behavior, Anthropic files for a massive IPO, and AI agents get more capable.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Microsoft offers devs a better way to control AI agent behavior
imageCaption: Microsoft offers devs a better way to control AI agent behavior
imageSourceTitle: Microsoft offers devs a better way to control AI agent behavior

body.text: I'm catching up on the latest AI news, and it's clear that AI agents are getting more capable by the day. But with great power comes great responsibility - or in this case, a big challenge for enterprises trying to ensure these agents behave as intended. I'm talking about the kind of situations where an AI agent is deployed across multiple applications, workflows, and products. It's a complex problem, but some companies are working on solutions.

In other news, Anthropic has confidentially filed for an initial public offering, which could be one of the largest IPOs ever. With a valuation of $965 billion, this is a significant move that could have major implications for the AI industry. I'm curious to see how this plays out, as it could be a sign of things to come for the AI sector.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
